The balance in the estate checking account exceeds the FDIC amount. [Probate Code Section 9700.] 3. An accounting cannot be approved if bond is insufficient. It appears that there was another request for special notice filed on 9/6/23 by Catherine Swafford for Withers Bergman, LLP, requesting special notice for all matters. She never got notice of the accounting/12200 petition filed on 9/25/23. Deadline is 6/7/24 for delivering courtesy copies of any new filings to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A supplement does not ordinarily require notice. [CRC 7.53.] However, the second supplement actually amended paragraph 10 of the petition to correct the allegation that all fees and costs have been paid. If filing a pleading form of proof of service is used, a copy of the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing must be attached to it, which Notice of Hearing shows all of the pleadings to be heard.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer To Cross-Petitioner Jennifer Sam'S First Amended Cross-Petition.(Added For Posting Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: {Part 3 of 4- Diana Sam} The determination of undue influence in procuring a testamentary instrument does not depend on any one element alone, but on the "probative force" of the elements in combination. (See, e.g., In re Lingenfelter's Estate (1952) 38 Cal.2d 571, 585; Estate of Baker (1982) 131 Cal.App.3d 471, 481.) Despite the FACP being replete with conclusory factual and legal statements and legal argument, overall, for the purposes of analysis on demurrer, it facially pleads facts, or facts arise from the face of the pleading by reasonable implication, sufficient to plead a right to relief under the undue influence, elder abuse, and breach of fiduciary duty causes of action. To wit, the FACP alleges that Petitioner held a confidential or fiduciary position relative to Decedent at the time the challenged trust was drafted and executed, due to Decedent at the relevant times being in the vulnerable position of advanced age, newly-widowed, non-English speaking, reliant on Petitioner for housing and other basic needs, and reliant on Petitioner's aid to wind down the family business and the marital finances that had exclusively been managed by his late wife; that Petitioner had an active role in the challenged trust's creation through that position, and thereby gained an undue or unfair position over Decedent, specifically in relation to the terms of the challenged trust, which names her as both a co-trustee and a beneficiary in contrast to the trusts Decedent executed before and after the challenged trust, which disinherit Petitioner. The question of a party's ability to prove the allegations, or the possible difficulty in making such proof, is not at issue on a demurrer. To survive demurrer, Cross-Petitioner is required only to plead facts showing entitlement to some relief; she is not required to present evidence. (Berry v. Frazier (2023) 90 Cal. App. 5th 1258, 1270; Brown v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2021) 60 Cal.App.5th 1092, 1103.) The Demurrer also challenges the sufficiency of the constructive fraud cause of action, on the same grounds as it challenges the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action; for the same reasons the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action survives the Demurrer on those grounds, the constructive fraud cause of action survives, too. The demurrer to the Fraud cause of action is, in part, also based on Petitioner's reading of Exhibit J and argument that it contradicts the FACP's allegations. As discussed above, when considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint, and judicial notice does not mean the court takes as notice of the veracity of hearsay statements contained in the court record. The FACP facially alleges Exhibit J supports its material allegations. Whether it will or won't went put under evidentiary scrutiny is not the question on demurrer. The court is not convinced by the Demurrer's citation to Lazar v. Superior Ct. (1996) 12 Cal. 4th 631, 638 that the necessary element of damages under a fraud cause of action must be pleaded as "recoverable damages." The Lazar court listed the elements of fraud "which give rise to the tort action for deceit, are (a) misrepresentation (false representation, concealment, or nondisclosure); (b) knowledge of falsity (or 'scienter'); (c) intent to defraud, i.e., to induce reliance; (d) justifiable reliance; and (e) resulting damage." (Ibid.) There is no showing that the element of "resulting damage" as used in Lazar is legally synonymous with "recoverable damages" as used by the Demurrer. The Lazar court then addressed specifically an action for "promissory fraud," which was relevant to the underlying claims of a defendant fraudulently inducing the plaintiff to (see part 4 of 4).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Diana Sam?S Notice Of Hearing And Demurrer To Cross-Petitioner Jennifer Sam?S First Amended Cross-Petition
rulings.Rulings: {Part 1 of 4 - Diana Sam} This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner Diana Sam's Demurrer to Cross-Petitioner Jennifer Sam's First Amended Cross-Petition. First Amended Cross-Petition Of Jennifer Sam To Determine Validity Of Trust Sections 1.1, 3.2 And 5.3(B) Of The Paul Sam Trust Dated November 5, 2019; For Elder Abuse; For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; For Constructive Fraud; And For Fraud ("FACP") was filed February 5, 2024. The causes of action are: First Cause of Action for Undue Influence; Second Cause of Action for Elder Abuse; Third Cause of Action for Breach of Fiduciary Duty; Fifth Cause of Action for Constructive Fraud. Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41(a) requires meet and confer conferences prior to filing a demurrer to be either in-person or telephonic. Per the declarations of Mr. Foster and Ms. Gamez, the attorneys engaged in an exchange of emails and did not meet either telephonically or in person. The Code requires parties to meet and confer in person or telephonically, and to do so in good faith. Rather than continuing the hearing on the Demurrer or ordering counsel to engage in compliant meet and confer efforts, the court will rule on the Demurrer now in the interests of judicial economy and avoiding further delay in this matter. (See, also, Code of Civil Proc. section 430.41(a)(4)). Counsel are instructed to follow statutory procedures henceforth. For the reasons discussed below, Petitioner Diana Sam's Demurrer to Cross-Petitioner Jennifer Sam's First Amended Cross-Petition is OVERRULED. Per the Notice of Hearing, the general demurrer is brought on the grounds that each of the causes of action in the FACP fails to state a cause of action. Per the Memorandum of Points and Authorities, the Undue Influence cause of action fails to state facts showing "influence" by Diana Sam over settlor-decedent's testamentary acts, and fails to state facts showing "undue" conduct by Diana. The Elder Abuse cause of action fails for the same reasons. The Breach of Fiduciary Duty and Constructive Fraud causes of action fail to plead a duty Diana owed to settlor-decedent and fail to show a breach of that alleged duty. The Fraud cause of action, like the cause of action for Undue Influence, also fails to state facts showing "influence" by Diana Sam over settlor-decedent's testamentary acts, and fails to state fact showing "undue" conduct by Diana, and fails to allege the element of recoverable damages. (MPA at 1:7-18.) A demurrer is a pleading used to challenge the legal sufficiency of an opponent's pleading based on defects that appear either on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318; County of Fresno v. Shelton (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 996, 1008, 1009.) When considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint and those arising by reasonable implication. (Moore v. Conliffe (1994) 7 Cal. 4th 634, 638; Montclair Parkowners Ass'n v. City of Montclair (1999) 76 Cal. App. 4th 784.) This does not apply to allegations expressing mere conclusions of law, or allegations contradicted by the exhibits to the complaint or by matters of which judicial notice may be taken. (Vance v. Villa Park Mobilehome Estates (1995) 36 Cal. App. 4th 698, 709.) Petitioner has submitted a multi-volume Request for Judicial Notice in connection with both this Demurrer and a concurrently-set Motion to Strike, consisting of court records in this matter. Cross-Petitioner has also submitted a Request for Judicial Notice, in support of the opposition to Demurrer, consisting of a court order issued in this matter, and definitions of words. (see part 2 of 4)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Objector Jennifer Sam?S First Amended Cross-Petition
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2 - Diana Sam] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Petitioner Diana Sam's Motion to Strike Objector Jennifer Sam's First Amended Cross-Petition. Per the Notice of Motion, Petitioner seeks an order striking Paragraphs 28, 41, and 43 of First Amended Cross-Petition Of Jennifer Sam To Determine Validity Of Trust Sections 1.1, 3.2 And 5.3(B) Of The Paul Sam Trust Dated November 5, 2019; For Elder Abuse; For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; For Constructive Fraud; And For Fraud ("FACP"), on the grounds that their falsity is shown by Exhibits J and K of the FACP, which are incorporated into the FACP. The Memorandum of Points and Authorities, but not the Notice of Motion, cites code of Civil Procedure sections 436 and 437 as authority for the Motion, arguing "a court may strike any false matter so long as its falsity appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter of which the court is required to take judicial notice." (MPA at 2:2-4.) Under Code of Civil Procedure section 436, The court may, upon a motion made pursuant to Section 435, or at any time in its discretion, and upon terms it deems proper: (a) Strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading. (b) Strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 437, (a) The grounds for a motion to strike shall appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter of which the court is required to take judicial notice. (b) Where the motion to strike is based on matter of which the court may take judicial notice pursuant to Section 452 or 453 of the Evidence Code, such matter shall be specified in the notice of motion, or in the supporting points and authorities, except as the court may otherwise permit. Petitioner Diana Sam's Motion to Strike Objector Jennifer Sam's First Amended Cross-Petition is DENIED, for the reasons discussed below. Petitioner has submitted a single multi-volume Request for Judicial Notice in connection with this Motion to Strike and a concurrently-set demurrer. Matters which must be judicially noticed are listed under Evid. Code section 451. None of the matters in this multi-volume request falls under Evidence Code section 451. The Request for Judicial Notice is brought pursuant to Evidence Code section 452, matters which "may" be judicially noticed. The Request for Judicial Notice contains court records, including Exhibits J and K of the FACP, on which the Motion relies heavily to argue that Paragraphs 28, 41, and 43 are false and should be stricken. Cross-Petitioner has also submitted a Request for Judicial Notice in opposition to the Motion. The court GRANTS both Petitioner's and Cross-Petitioner's Request for Judicial Notice. However, the court also reminds counsel that "Judicial notice" is the court's recognition of the existence of a matter of law or fact, without the necessity of formal proof of the existence of a matter of law or fact. (See Evid. C. section 450 et seq.) Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in those materials. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) In short, the court is taking judicial notice of the existence of Exhibits J and K, not the veracity of the hearsay statements they contain. (See, Garcia v. Sterling (1985) 176 Cal. App. 3d 17, 21-22, quoting Day v. Sharp (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 904, 914, emphasis in original.) (See part 2 of 2)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Strike Objector Jennifer Sam'S First Amended Cross-Petition (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2 - Diana Sam] Exhibits J and K of the FACP are, respectively, an email sent by attorney Richard Shu to Petitioner on October 15, 2019, and an August 5, 2019 Invoice from the law firm of Barulich Dugoni & Suttmann, the law firm that drafted the challenged trust. The FACP includes them as exhibits, and incorporates their contents into its allegations. The Motion to Strike argues that these documents actually signify and support the opposite of what the FACP alleges, or at the very least, do not support the FACP's allegations regarding Petitioner's authority and control over the challenged trust's drafting. Both of these documents are hearsay statements. The Court has taken judicial notice of their existence, but cannot take judicial notice of the truth of facts asserted in these documents; they are not "documents such as orders, findings of fact and conclusions of law, and judgments." (Garcia, supra, 176 Cal. App. 3d at 21.) Whether either of these documents, and the matters asserted therein, will fall within a hearsay exception and become evidence in the course of time and trial, or be successfully rebutted or disproved, is not the inquiry on a motion to strike. On a motion to strike, the court assumes the challenged factual allegations of the pleading are true, and reads them in the context of the pleading. Paragraphs 28, 41, and 43 of the FACP contain factual allegations, made in the context of the pleading's allegations of elder abuse, undue influence, breach of fiduciary duty, constructive fraud, and fraud: Paragraph 28 alleges several facts about Petitioner retaining the drafting law firm, about Petitioner's and her husband's professional lives, and their alleged participation in obtaining the challenged trust. Paragraph 41 is summary of and quotes from the Exhibit K Invoice. Paragraph 43 is a summary of and quotations from the Exhibit J email. These paragraphs survive the Motion to Strike brought under Code of Civil Procedure sections 436 and 437, as their alleged falsity is not apparent from either the face of the pleading, or from any matter subject to mandatory judicial notice under Evidence Code section 451, or on the basis of a matter subject to discretionary judicial notice. Cross-Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. (end of tentative ruling)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer To Cross-Petitioner Jennifer Sam'S First Amended Cross-Petition.(Added For Posting Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: {Part 2 of 4- Diana Sam} The court GRANTS both Petitioner's and Cross-Petitioner's requests for judicial notice. "Judicial notice" is the court's recognition of the existence of a matter of law or fact, without the necessity of formal proof of the existence of a matter of law or fact. (See Evid. C. section 450 et seq.) Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in those materials. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) "A court may take judicial notice of the existence of each document in a court file, but can only take judicial notice of the truth of facts asserted in documents such as orders, findings of fact and conclusions of law, and judgments.[citation.] [ ] A mistaken notion exists that taking judicial notice of court records means noticing " '... the existence of facts asserted in [all the documents in] a court file ... a court cannot take judicial notice of hearsay allegations as being true, just because they are part of a court record or file....' " (Garcia v. Sterling (1985) 176 Cal. App. 3d 17, 21-22, quoting Day v. Sharp (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 904, 914, emphasis in original.) Of the court records subject to judicial notice, the Demurrer relies especially on Exhibit J of the attorney declaration filed in support of the FACP, an email sent by attorney Richard Shu to Petitioner on October 15, 2019. The FACP incorporates this email by reference. The Demurrer argues the basis of the FACP's undue influence and elder abuse claims is actually directly contradicted by this email. While Petitioner presents argument regarding the veracity of allegations presented in the FACP and what Petitioner claims is actually being discussed in Exhibit J, that is not the relevant analytical exercise on a demurrer. When considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint and those arising by reasonable implication. (Moore v. Conliffe (1994) 7 Cal.4th 634, 638; Montclair Parkowners Ass'n v. City of Montclair (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 784.) (see part 3 of 4)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PAUL SAM TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 5, 2019, AS RESTATED on March 18, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer To Cross-Petitioner Jennifer Sam'S First Amended Cross-Petition.(Added For Posting Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: {Part 4 of 4- Diana Sam} enter into a contract. The FACP does not facially plead a claim for promissory fraud. Read as a whole, for the purposes of analysis on demurrer, the FACP alleges that Petitioner intentionally and knowingly obtained the terms of the challenged Trust without Decedent's awareness or consent, to her own advantage and contrary to Decedent's testamentary intent to retain control over administration of the trust and disposition of trust assets, and damages to Decedent arose out of that abrogation of intent. Whether Cross-Petitioner will be able to support the FACP's claims with sufficient evidence, including Exhibit J, and whether Objectors will be able to rebut or defend against these claims with their own showing of sufficient evidence, is not before the court on a demurrer. Cross-Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. (end of tentative ruling).

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order 1)Removing Trustee & Surcharge 2)Compelling Trustee To Account 3)Reducing Trustee'S Use Of Trust Funds For Litigation And 5)Awarding Damages And Attorneys' Fee And Costs For Financial Elder Abuse

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERARDO SORRENTINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Paragraph 3h of the report is not quite accurate, however, as the executor has not performed all duties required of him if a Supplemental I&A will still need to be filed. Notwithstanding Executor's request made on 3/5/24 for just 8 more weeks to file his first and final account and petition for final distribution, which would have preceded the date of this hearing, the status of closing the estate will be set for 7/1/24. A petition for final distribution or a further report of status must be filed by 6/17/24. Appearance will be required on 7/1/24 if no petition is timely filed. (No SASE was provided.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAY EARL GOODMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? When did Carl Goodman die? (David Goodman and Charles Goodman are presumably the children of Carl Goodman?) (2) A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] (3) Why, if Lisa Kim is a person "who may be entitled to notice," was she not listed in item 8 of the petition with her relationship to the decedent, age, and address? Deadline is 5/30/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (After procedural issues are addressed, an appearance might still be required, as the JC form 147S provided a passport number, not a driver's license number.)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MICHAEL JOSEPH CALONICO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF BIRTH
rulings.Rulings: *REPOSTED 5/6/24 at 10:07 a.m.* Continued to 5/22/2024. The following issues to be cleared by 5/10/2024: (1) Attorney's signature on the petition. (2) The caption (title) of the petition/case must be the name of the person whose fact of birth, not of the petitioner. (3) Petitioner's verified supplement clarifying whether Michael Joseph Calonico and Michaelem Calonico are the same person. The issue (1) and (2) can be cleared by an amended petition. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
